text: Robotic 8mm trocars are placed in a straight line from the left upper quadrant to the right lower quadrant, with an additional trocar in the left lower quadrant. The surgeon begins dissection by exploring the ileocolic pedicle and opening the ileocolic window.

The surgeon isolates the ileocolic vessels from the superior mesenteric artery and vein, ligating them with Hem-o-lok clips before dividing them. Mesenteric dissection is performed using a medial to lateral approach, maintaining dissection between the embryological planes anterior to the adenoma. Subsequently, the right colic artery is dissected, ligated, and divided.

The surgeon identifies, ligates, and divides the right branch of the middle colic artery. The lateral attachments of the colon are taken down, completing the mesenteric dissection. The transverse colon is then prepared and transected using a robotic stapler.

The surgeon prepares the terminal ileum for transection, using a robotic stapler to perform the transection. A stay suture is placed to facilitate the side-to-side anastomosis. Small incisions are made in the transverse colon and terminal ileum for the insertion of the robotic linear stapler. The side-to-side ileocolic anastomosis is created intracorporeally, and the opening in the anastomosis is closed with a 3-0 Vicryl suture.

The surgeon places the resected specimen into an endobag and extracts it through a five-centimeter suprapubic incision. The intact specimen is displayed, and the distance between the tumor and the high vascular tie is measured.
